sections.paragraph:
Ἁνανίας, ου, ὁ also Ἀ- [Heb.] Ananias

—1. a Christian in Jerusalem who connived with his wife Sapphira to divert money devoted to God Ac 5:1.

—2. a Christian in Damascus who instructed Paul and baptized him Ac 9:10; 22:12.

—3. an Israelite high priest, son of Nebedeus, in office c. 47–59, Ac 23:2; 24:1.
